unexpected_finds.finding: Phase 0 ETHICS-GATE blocked Loday-Richaud / Mitschi citation as load-bearing because the PDF was not on disk in the runbook-canonical literature directory. v2.1 keeps the bib entry (carry-forward from v2) but removes the load-bearing \cite in §5(d) and replaces it with a prose acknowledgement that the volume was not consulted at v2.1 preparation time. This is recorded transparently in the manuscript and in rubber_duck_critique.md item 7.
unexpected_finds.action_taken: Removed \cite{lodayrichaud2016divergent} from §5(d); kept Costin 2008 ch.5 §5.0a as the modern Borel-Laplace see-also reinforcement.
unexpected_finds.downstream_owner: operator (writing task for v2.2 if Loday-Richaud volume PDF is sourced)

unexpected_finds.finding: v2.1 PDF lands at exactly 9 pages, the lower edge of the FULL band [9, 12] in Phase E.10. Within band; no halt, but worth noting.
unexpected_finds.action_taken: None. Recommendations in rubber_duck_critique.md items 6 / 1, 2, 4 list candidate paragraphs that would push to ~10 pp if a v2.2 polish pass is desired.
unexpected_finds.downstream_owner: Claude review / future operator polish pass

unexpected_finds.finding: The pre-existing bib entry birkhofftrjitzinsky1932analytic in annotated_bibliography.bib lists year=1932, but the canonical Acta Mathematica 60 record is 1933 (DOI 10.1007/BF02398269 publication year 1933). The new birkhoff_trjitzinsky_1933 entry added in v2.1 uses the canonical 1933 year. The 1932 entry is left untouched (unused by v2.1) for backward compatibility with v2.
unexpected_finds.action_taken: Added new birkhoff_trjitzinsky_1933 entry with year=1933; v2.1 \cite calls reference the new key.
